generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatBedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heating element delivering at least 100W of power within a closed-loop control system to maintain target temperatures in the incubator. Operates under software controller supervision with safety constraints including overcurrent protection and temperature thresholds."
]

instance IncubatorFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Airflow regulation component providing approximately 0.02 m³/s airflow to enhance thermal distribution inside the insulated styrofoam box. Governed by software controller interfacing with the Digital Twin via real-time communication."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 digital temperature sensor monitoring internal temperature of the incubator box with ±0.5°C accuracy and 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C). Positioned to capture thermal gradients within the insulated styrofoam container."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 digital temperature sensor monitoring internal temperature of the incubator box with ±0.5°C accuracy and 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C). Positioned to capture thermal gradients within the insulated styrofoam container."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 digital temperature sensor measuring external ambient temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy and 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C). Provides environmental context for thermal model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataStream1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataStream2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance CombinedSensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureDataStream1, InternalTemperatureDataStream2, ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified differential equation-based thermal model with two free parameters representing dynamic thermal behavior of the incubator. Used for quick temperature predictions and control law adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, DifferentialEquationSolver
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Detailed differential equation-based thermal model with four free parameters capturing thermal inertia and complex heat transfer dynamics. Provides high-precision temperature predictions for advanced control scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, DifferentialEquationSolver
]

instance GeometricIncubatorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric representation of the incubator's insulated styrofoam box, heatbed, and fan components. Captures physical dimensions and structural attributes without dynamic interactions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance RawTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed temperature readings from all three DS18S20 sensors (two internal, one external) collected via 1-Wire protocol. Includes timestamped raw values without noise filtering or alignment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CombinedSensorDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, StateEstimationAlgorithm
]

instance CalibratedModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Refined thermal model parameters (thermal conductivity, heat loss coefficients) derived from calibration algorithms comparing simulated and observed sensor data. Used to maintain accurate representation of the Physical Twin's state."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CombinedSensorDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DifferentialEquationSolver, FaultDetectionService
]

instance PredictedTemperatureTrajectories : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Future temperature projections generated by calibrated differential equation solvers under current or hypothetical operating conditions. Enables what-if analysis and proactive control adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CalibratedModelParameters
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler, AdaptiveControlService
]

instance ControlSignals : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized actuation commands (heatbed duty cycles, fan speeds) generated by model-predictive control algorithms to maintain target temperatures while minimizing energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PredictedTemperatureTrajectories
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationInterfaceService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central hardware platform acting as the control unit interfacing with sensors via 1-Wire protocol and actuators via real-time communication. Hosts RabbitMQ server for message synchronization and runs low-level drivers for data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, StateEstimationService, AdaptiveControlService
]

instance RabbitMQServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asynchronous message broker ensuring real-time data flow between Physical Twin sensors, controllers, and Digital Twin processing modules. Facilitates low-latency communication for temperature sensor data and control signals."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService, ModelCalibrationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance PythonScipyPackage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization library utilized for parameter tuning, control law adjustments, and model-predictive control algorithms within the Digital Twin's algorithmic layer."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, PredictiveControlService, AdaptiveControlService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic process updating cyber-physical models with real-time sensor data to improve accuracy for simulation and prediction. Compares simulated and observed temperature data to refine model parameters."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateEstimationService, ModelValidationService
]

instance DifferentialEquationSolver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Numerical solver integrated into the Digital Twin's simulation engine to model dynamic thermal behavior (heat transfer, thermal inertia) of the incubator. Executes both two-parameter and four-parameter thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance StateEstimationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic component refining model parameters (thermal conductivity, heat loss coefficients) by comparing simulated and observed sensor data. Ensures accurate representation of the Physical Twin's state for control purposes."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Graphical interface component presenting real-time and historical data (temperature trends, control actions) to engineers for system diagnosis and model validation. Facilitates cross-disciplinary collaboration."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, ModelValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous acquisition and synchronization of temperature sensor data (internal/external) via Raspberry Pi's data layer. Provides up-to-date system state for monitoring and control applications."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureInsight, SystemStateInsight
]

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service refining thermal model parameters through calibration algorithms to ensure accurate representation of the Physical Twin's state. Compares simulated and observed data to detect parameter drift."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterDriftInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of dynamic thermal models to simulate the Physical Twin's response under current or hypothetical operating conditions. Enables behavioral reproduction for what-if analysis scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureSimulationInsight, ControlScenarioInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Projection of future system states (temperature trajectories) using calibrated models. Supports proactive decision-making through predictive temperature forecasts."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureForecastInsight, ProactiveAdjustmentInsight
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generation of optimized control signals (heatbed duty cycles, fan speeds) via model-predictive control algorithms to maintain target temperatures while minimizing energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction, TemperatureControlAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of supervisory controllers and fault detectors to identify deviations from optimal performance (sensor drift, actuator failures) by cross-referencing monitored data with model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, AnomalyAlertAction
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of system conditions to flag violations of predefined thresholds (e.g., temperature exceeding safe limits). Triggers alerts or automated corrective actions when safety constraints are breached."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationInsight, EmergencyAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Presentation of real-time and historical data (temperature trends, control actions) via graphical interfaces. Aids engineers in diagnosing issues and validating model accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataVisualizationInsight, ModelValidationInsight
]

instance ActuationInterfaceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interface component translating control signals from the Digital Twin into physical actuation commands for the incubator's heatbed and fan. Ensures proper communication between software controllers and hardware actuators."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureControlAction
]

instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for acquiring and aggregating raw sensor data from the Physical Twin's temperature sensors. Ensures data integrity and synchronization for model calibration and monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service updating and refining thermal models using real-time sensor data. Applies optimization algorithms to minimize discrepancies between simulated and observed temperature data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterDriftInsight
]

instance PredictiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating optimized control signals using predictive models to maintain target temperatures while minimizing energy consumption. Implements model-predictive control strategies."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service comparing simulated and observed system behavior to validate the accuracy of Digital Twin models. Identifies discrepancies requiring model updates or recalibration."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuously updated temperature readings from all three sensors (two internal, one external) synchronized with the Physical Twin's state. Provides foundation for monitoring and control decisions."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive representation of the incubator's current operational state including temperature gradients, thermal inertia effects, and environmental conditions."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative measure of the calibrated thermal models' accuracy compared to real-world sensor data. Indicates how well the Digital Twin represents the Physical Twin's behavior."
]

instance ParameterDriftInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of deviations in model parameters (thermal conductivity, heat loss coefficients) over time, indicating potential changes in the Physical Twin's characteristics or sensor inaccuracies."
]

instance TemperatureSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated temperature trajectories under current operating conditions or hypothetical scenarios. Enables analysis of system behavior without physical intervention."
]

instance ControlScenarioInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted system response to different control inputs (heatbed power, fan speed) under various environmental conditions. Supports optimization of control strategies."
]

instance TemperatureForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projections of future temperature states based on calibrated models and current operating conditions. Enables proactive adjustments to maintain target temperatures."
]

instance ProactiveAdjustmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for preemptive control actions (e.g., modifying heating profiles) to maintain target temperatures before deviations become critical."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of anomalies in sensor data or actuator performance compared to expected model behavior. Indicates potential hardware failures or environmental disturbances."
]

instance AnomalyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert generation when deviations from normal operation are detected. Notifies operators or maintenance personnel of potential issues requiring attention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of control parameters to minimize energy consumption while maintaining target temperatures. Implements model-predictive control strategies."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation and execution of control signals (heatbed duty cycles, fan speeds) to maintain precise temperature regulation according to calibrated models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated corrective measures triggered when safety thresholds are violated (e.g., temperature exceeding safe limits). May include shutting down heating elements or activating cooling systems."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical representation of real-time and historical temperature data, control actions, and model predictions. Facilitates system diagnosis and model validation by engineers."
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison between simulated and observed system behavior to validate the accuracy of Digital Twin models. Identifies discrepancies requiring model updates or recalibration."
]

instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of system conditions violating predefined safety thresholds (e.g., temperature exceeding safe limits). Provides critical information for triggering emergency actions."
]

// C1: Environment and Components
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InsulatedContainerComponent, HeatBedComponent, FanComponent, TemperatureSensingSubsystem
]

instance InsulatedContainerComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to maintain internal temperature stability. Acts as the primary containment structure for the incubator's regulated environment."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance HeatBedComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Primary heating element delivering controlled thermal energy to the incubator's internal environment. Operates within closed-loop control to maintain target temperatures."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance FanComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Airflow regulation device ensuring uniform temperature distribution within the insulated container. Enhances thermal performance by mitigating hot/cold spots."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance TemperatureSensingSubsystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Sensing infrastructure comprising three DS18S20 temperature sensors (two internal, one external) with ±0.5°C accuracy. Provides real-time thermal data for model calibration and control."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) of the incubator system generates control commands for the Physical Twin (PT) via the Raspberry Pi, which acts as the central controller. The DT issues commands to adjust the **heatbed** and **fan** based on deviations detected in the calibrated models or violations in system performance (e.g., temperature drift or safety thresholds). These commands are executed directly by the Raspberry Pi, which modulates power to the heatbed and activates/deactivates the fan to maintain the desired internal temperature. The system includes a **feedback loop** where the PT's sensor data (internal/external temperatures) is continuously monitored, and adjustments are made dynamically. If violations (e.g., overheating or underperformance) are detected, the DT triggers corrective actions, either through automated algorithms or human intervention, as depicted in the schematic overview of the DT framework (Figure 1). No explicit validation/authentication mechanisms or conflict resolution protocols are described in the documents.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator employs a periodic synchronization mechanism between the Physical Twin (PT) and the Digital Twin (DT), where a low-level driver collects temperature data from the PT's sensors (two internal and one external) at unspecified but likely regular intervals. The calibration algorithms then update the DT's models (e.g., differential equation-based thermal models with 2 or 4 free parameters) to reflect the PT's current state. While the exact synchronization frequency is not quantified, the system implies a feedback loop involving real-time or near-real-time monitoring and control, as the DT must continuously adjust to maintain the target temperature inside the insulated container. The trade-off between model accuracy (e.g., the 4-parameter model capturing thermal inertia more precisely) and computational speed (e.g., slower simulation for higher-fidelity models) is acknowledged, but no explicit latency or granularity requirements are specified in the documents. The system relies on a Raspberry Pi for processing, which suggests a time scale suitable for low-latency control applications, though no hard constraints (e.g., <100ms–1s) are provided."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **cyber-physical system (CPS)-based architecture**, integrating three core components: **Data Acquisition**, **Model Calibration**, and **Monitoring & Control**. The **Data Acquisition** layer is implemented via a **low-level driver** that periodically reads temperature data from the PT's sensors (two internal and one external) and forwards it to the **Communication Server** (a **RabbitMQ server**) for synchronization. The **Model Calibration** layer employs **calibration algorithms** to update the DT's thermal models (e.g., differential equation-based models with 2 or 4 parameters) using the incoming sensor data, ensuring the DT's predictions align with the PT's observed behavior. The **Monitoring & Control** layer uses the calibrated models to inform a **temperature controller** (running on the Raspberry Pi) that adjusts the heatbed and fan to maintain the target temperature. The system also includes a **supervisory mechanism** to detect violations (e.g., unsafe or suboptimal performance) and trigger corrective actions. The architecture is **centralized**, with the Raspberry Pi serving as the sole computational node, and relies on **RabbitMQ** for inter-component communication and synchronization."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of the Digital Twin (DT) system described in the documents follows a structured approach integrating **development stages**, **representation types**, and **transition management** between phases. The lifecycle begins with **modeling** (e.g., CAD models for geometric aspects like wheel sizes or chassis shapes, and dynamic models using differential equations for behavior analysis), which are later integrated into the DT framework. During the **deployment phase**, the DT is operationalized alongside the Physical Twin (PT), leveraging **calibration algorithms** to continuously update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment with real-time sensor data. This ensures the DT remains synchronized with the PT's current state. The lifecycle also includes **monitoring** via algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, supervisory controllers) to assess system performance, detect violations, and trigger corrective actions. **Lifecycle governance** is implied through iterative updates, where human or algorithmic interventions adjust the PT based on DT insights, ensuring adaptive robustness. The documents emphasize that models are not discarded post-deployment but are **continuously refined** to maintain accuracy and relevance, with trade-offs between abstraction levels and simulation speed acknowledged (e.g., accurate models may be computationally intensive)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s **model fidelity** and **validity** are critical for ensuring accurate representation and decision-making. Key considerations include:
  - **Model fidelity requirements**:
    - **Complexity vs. accuracy trade-offs**: The incubator case study demonstrates this via two thermal models—one with **2 free parameters** (simpler) and another with **4 free parameters** (more complex). The latter was selected for its **higher fidelity** in predicting thermal inertia, a critical dynamic property.
    - **Domain-specific needs**: Models must capture **physics-based behaviors** (e.g., differential equations for car dynamics or heat transfer in the incubator) while abstracting non-essential details.
  - **Validation and verification methods**:
    - **Experimental validation**: The incubator study used **real-world experiments** to compare model predictions (e.g., temperature trajectories) against actual PT behavior, identifying the 4-parameter model as superior.
    - **Calibration against sensor data**: Models are iteratively refined using **real-time sensor inputs** (e.g., temperature readings) to minimize prediction errors.
  - **Uncertainty sources and quantification**:
    - **Parameter uncertainty**: Free parameters in models (e.g., thermal conductivity in the incubator) introduce variability; the study implies that **higher-parameter models reduce uncertainty** in critical dynamics (e.g., thermal inertia).
    - **Sensor noise and environmental variability**: Not explicitly quantified but acknowledged as potential sources of error in model inputs.
  - **Error detection and correction mechanisms**:
    - **Monitoring algorithms**: The DT employs **monitors** to detect deviations between predicted and actual PT behavior (e.g., temperature spikes in the incubator).
    - **Feedback loops**: Calibration algorithms adjust model parameters in response to sensor data, ensuring alignment with the PT’s current state.
  - **Quality assurance processes**:
    - **Iterative refinement**: Models are continuously updated based on **experimental results** and **operational data** (e.g., the incubator’s open-source hardware/software allows for community-driven validation).
    - **Cross-model validation**: Comparing simpler vs. complex models (e.g., 2-parameter vs. 4-parameter) to select the most appropriate representation for the DT’s use case."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) of the incubator system relies on a **local network infrastructure** centered around the Raspberry Pi, which hosts the **Rabbit MQ server** for message brokering between sensors, models, and controllers. Sensor data is transmitted via a **1-Wire bus**, a low-speed, single-wire communication protocol optimized for temperature sensors. The DT framework (Figure 1) implies a **closed-loop architecture** where data flows from sensors to models, then to monitors, and finally to controllers. No explicit mention of broader network topologies (e.g., Ethernet, Wi-Fi) or security protocols (e.g., encryption) is provided in the documents. The system prioritizes **real-time responsiveness** due to the periodic sensor readings and immediate control actions required for temperature regulation.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) of the incubator system is **hosted exclusively on the Raspberry Pi**, which serves as both the computational and control unit for the Physical Twin (PT). The deployment infrastructure is **edge-based**, with no indication of cloud or hybrid hosting. The system's computational requirements are minimal, focusing on real-time sensor data processing, model calibration, and control logic execution. Storage needs are limited to retaining sensor data and model parameters temporarily for predictive analysis. The document explicitly states that **detailed implementation of the DT is out of scope**, and no scalability mechanisms, deployment models (e.g., containerization), or redundancy strategies are described. The Raspberry Pi ensures **localized availability** and **low-latency operation**, critical for the incubator's temperature regulation tasks.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety monitoring mechanisms** as part of its operational framework. Specifically, the DT employs **monitors** that continuously assess whether the **Physical Twin (PT)**—the incubator system—is performing **safely and optimally** under its current operating conditions. These monitors rely on **real-time data** collected from sensors (e.g., two internal temperature sensors and one external temperature sensor) and **calibrated models** of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment. While the document does not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems**, it implies a foundational reliance on **data-driven safety checks** to detect deviations or violations in system behavior. The absence of explicit mention of **data protection compliance**, **anonymization**, or **consent management** suggests these aspects are not addressed in the provided context. The system’s safety-critical considerations are inferred from the **calibration algorithms** used to update models and the **supervisory role** of monitors in triggering corrective actions (e.g., human or algorithmic interventions) when the PT deviates from safe operation."
]
